# rhcs_cluster_osd_gcp (Resource)

OpenShift Dedicated (OSD) cluster on Google Cloud Platform. CCS clusters
require either `wif_config_id` (Workload Identity Federation) or
`gcp_authentication` (service account key).

This resource composes four advanced features when configured together:

* **WIF** (`wif_config_id`) — Workload Identity Federation, no long-lived
  service account keys.
* **CMEK** (`gcp_encryption_key`) — Customer-managed encryption keys for
  etcd and worker disks.
* **PSC** (`private_service_connect`) — Private Service Connect for an
  internal-only cluster API endpoint.
* **Shared VPC** (`gcp_network.vpc_project_id`) — Two-project network
  topology (host project owns the VPC, service project runs the cluster).

A cluster-admin htpasswd user can be created inline via `admin_credentials`
or `create_admin_user`, mirroring [rhcs_cluster_rosa_classic](cluster_rosa_classic.md);
no separate identity-provider resource is required for the bootstrap admin.

A full runbook-style example, including the GCP-side WIF IAM via the
`hashicorp/google` provider, is in
`examples/create_osd_gcp_cluster_full/`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "rhcs_wif_config" "wif" {
  display_name      = "my-cluster-wif"
  openshift_version = "4.21.15"
  gcp = {
    project_id     = "my-gcp-project"
    project_number = "123456789012"
    role_prefix    = "mycluster"
  }
}

resource "rhcs_cluster_osd_gcp" "osd" {
  name           = "my-cluster"
  product        = "osd"
  cloud_region   = "us-east1"
  gcp_project_id = "my-gcp-project"
  wif_config_id  = rhcs_wif_config.wif.id
  version        = "4.21.15"
  compute_nodes  = 3
  ccs_enabled    = true

  # Inline cluster-admin htpasswd user. Mirrors rhcs_cluster_rosa_classic.
  # Leave password unset to have the provider auto-generate one (exposed via
  # the admin_credentials attribute).
  create_admin_user = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cloud_region` (String) GCP region (e.g., us-central1).
- `gcp_project_id` (String) GCP project ID for the cluster.
- `name` (String) Name of the cluster.

### Optional

- `admin_credentials` (Attributes) Admin user credentials created with the cluster (htpasswd identity provider). Cannot be changed after cluster creation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--admin_credentials))
- `autoscaling` (Object) Autoscaling configuration for worker nodes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--autoscaling))
- `availability_zones` (List of String) GCP availability zones for the cluster. When specified: must be exactly 1 zone for single-AZ (multi_az = false), or exactly 3 zones for multi-AZ (multi_az = true). Omit to let OCM choose. Ensure the compute machine type is available in each zone (e.g. bare-metal types are zone-specific).
- `billing_model` (String) Billing model for the cluster. For CCS clusters, defaults to 'marketplace-gcp'. Set to 'standard' to use standard billing. Only 'standard' and 'marketplace-gcp' are allowed.
- `ccs_enabled` (Boolean) Enable Customer Cloud Subscription (CCS) mode.
- `compute_machine_type` (String) GCP machine type for worker nodes (e.g., custom-4-16384). Defaults to n2-standard-4 when not specified.
- `compute_nodes` (Number) Number of worker nodes (when not using autoscaling).
- `create_admin_user` (Boolean) Set to true to create a cluster-admin user with a generated password (username defaults to 'cluster-admin'). Ignored if admin_credentials is set. Cannot be changed after cluster creation.
- `domain_prefix` (String) DNS domain prefix for the cluster.
- `gcp_authentication` (Attributes) GCP service account authentication (when not using WIF). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gcp_authentication))
- `gcp_encryption_key` (Attributes) Customer-managed encryption key (CMEK) for CCS clusters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gcp_encryption_key))
- `gcp_network` (Object) GCP network configuration for BYO VPC. Set vpc_project_id only for Shared VPC (host project differs from cluster project). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gcp_network))
- `marketplace_gcp_terms` (Boolean) Whether GCP marketplace terms have been accepted.
- `multi_az` (Boolean) Deploy across multiple availability zones.
- `network` (Object) Network CIDR configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--network))
- `private` (Boolean) Restrict the cluster API endpoint and application routes to private (internal) connectivity only. When true, the OCM API server listening method is set to 'internal'. Requires a BYO VPC (gcp_network) and Private Service Connect (private_service_connect). Cannot be changed after cluster creation.
- `private_service_connect` (Object) Private Service Connect configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--private_service_connect))
- `product` (String) Product type (default: osd). Allowed values: osd, osdtrial.
- `properties` (Map of String) Cluster properties.
- `proxy` (Object) Proxy configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--proxy))
- `security` (Object) GCP security settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--security))
- `version` (String) OpenShift version (e.g., 4.16.1).
- `wait_for_create_complete` (Boolean) Wait for cluster to be ready after creation. Defaults to true.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) Timeout in minutes for cluster create and delete wait. Defaults to 60.
- `wif_config_id` (String) ID of the WIF config (when using Workload Identity Federation). Best practice: use one WIF config per cluster.
- `wif_verify_timeout_minutes` (Number) When using wif_config_id, wait up to this many minutes for WIF config verification before creating the cluster. GCP IAM propagation can take several minutes. Default 10.

### Read-Only

- `api_url` (String) API server URL.
- `console_url` (String) Web console URL.
- `current_compute` (Number) Current number of compute nodes.
- `domain` (String) Cluster domain.
- `external_id` (String) External identifier of the cluster.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the cluster (from OCM).
- `infra_id` (String) Infrastructure ID.
- `state` (String) Current state of the cluster.

### Nested Schema for `admin_credentials`

Optional:

- `password` (String, Sensitive) Admin password that will be created with the cluster.
- `username` (String) Admin username that will be created with the cluster.

- `private_key_id` (String)

- `auth_provider_x509_cert_url` (String)
- `auth_uri` (String)
- `client_x509_cert_url` (String)
- `token_uri` (String)
